questions.question: A speed–time graph for an object of mass $5.0\text{ kg}$ shows the speed increasing from $0\text{ m/s}$ to $12\text{ m/s}$ in $4.0\text{ s}$. Calculate the resultant force acting on the object.
questions.explanation:
Acceleration $a = (12 - 0) / 4 = 3.0\text{ m/s}^2$. Resultant force $F = ma = 5.0 \times 3.0 = 15\text{ N}$. 

**Common mistake:** Multiplying mass by final speed ($5 \times 12 = 60$) instead of by acceleration.

questions.question: An object moves in a circular path. If the resultant force on the object increases while the object continues moving at the same speed, what must happen to its motion?
options: It must move with a smaller radius
It must move with a larger radius
It will move in a straight line
questions.explanation:
According to the note, if the resultant force increases and speed remains constant, the object must follow a path with a smaller radius. 

**Common mistake:** Thinking a larger force 'pushes' the object further out into a larger circle.

questions.question: A resultant force acts on a moving object in a direction that is perpendicular to its velocity. What effect does this force have?
options: It changes the direction of motion but not the speed
It increases the speed of the object
It decreases the speed of the object
It has no effect on the motion of the object
questions.explanation:
A perpendicular force changes the direction of the velocity vector (turning the object) but does not change the magnitude (speed). 

**Common mistake:** Believing a perpendicular force has 'no effect' on motion at all.

questions.question: An object of mass $1.0\text{ kg}$ falls through air. At a certain instant, the air resistance is $4.0\text{ N}$. Calculate the acceleration of the object at this instant. (Take $g = 10\text{ m/s}^2$)
questions.explanation:
Weight $W = mg = 1.0 \times 10 = 10\text{ N}$. Resultant force $F_{net} = 10\text{ N} - 4\text{ N} = 6\text{ N}$. Acceleration $a = F_{net} / m = 6 / 1 = 6.0\text{ m/s}^2$. 

**Common mistake:** Using only the air resistance force ($4.0$) to calculate acceleration.

questions.question: A lorry is pulled by two jeeps. Each jeep exerts a force of $3000\text{ N}$ at an angle of $45^{circ}$ to the horizontal forward direction. What is the total resultant force pulling the lorry forward?
questions.explanation:
Each jeep contributes a forward component of $3000 \times cos(45^{circ}) = 2121.3\text{ N}$. Total force $= 2121.3 \times 2 = 4242.6\text{ N}$ (\approx $4243\text{ N}$). 

**Common mistake:** Simple addition ($6000\text{ N}$), which ignores the fact that the forces are not parallel.
